_meta.purpose: Auto-generate personalized edge case checklist based on input type and constraints
_meta.usage: Stage 6 — after approach is identified, before coding
_meta.howToUse: Select input types that match your problem. Universal cases always apply. Type-specific cases apply based on selection.

universalCases.whyItMatters: Most algorithms assume at least 2 elements. Single element often breaks loop logic, comparisons, and base cases.
universalCases.checkQuestion: Does your solution return correct answer when input has exactly one element?
universalCases.commonFailure: Off-by-one in loop bounds. Comparing element to itself.

universalCases.whyItMatters: Smallest case where relationships between elements exist. Catches swap logic, pair comparison bugs.
universalCases.checkQuestion: Does your solution handle exactly two elements correctly in all orderings?
universalCases.commonFailure: Hardcoded assumptions about having at least 3 elements.

universalCases.whyItMatters: Stress tests time and memory. Catches TLE and MLE that small inputs hide.
universalCases.checkQuestion: Does your solution finish within time limit at maximum n? Does memory stay within limit?
universalCases.commonFailure: Solution passes small tests but TLEs at n = 10^5 due to hidden O(n^2) loop.

universalCases.whyItMatters: Breaks solutions that assume distinct values. Causes issues with strict vs non-strict comparisons.
universalCases.checkQuestion: What does your solution return when all elements are the same value?
universalCases.commonFailure: Greedy or two-pointer solutions that rely on finding different elements to make progress.

universalCases.whyItMatters: Opposite extreme from all-same. Verifies solution handles no duplicate scenario.
universalCases.checkQuestion: Does solution work correctly when no two elements share a value?
universalCases.commonFailure: Solutions that assume duplicates exist for their logic to work.

universalCases.whyItMatters: Pre-sorted input can cause worst-case behavior in some algorithms (naive quicksort). Can also simplify or break certain approaches.
universalCases.checkQuestion: Does your solution handle already-sorted input correctly and efficiently?
universalCases.commonFailure: Naive quicksort degrades to O(n^2). Greedy picks wrong element when all are in order.

universalCases.whyItMatters: Reverse-sorted is the other common worst case. Catches reverse iteration bugs.
universalCases.checkQuestion: Does your solution handle reverse-sorted input correctly?
universalCases.commonFailure: Binary search on wrong half. Two pointer moving in wrong direction.

universalCases.whyItMatters: Solutions often return wrong default when no valid answer exists. Empty result needs explicit handling.
universalCases.checkQuestion: What does your solution return when the answer is 0, empty, or impossible?
universalCases.commonFailure: Returning uninitialized variable. Returning -1 when 0 is the valid answer.

universalCases.whyItMatters: Boundary of answer range. Catches overflow in accumulation.
universalCases.checkQuestion: Does your solution correctly compute and return the maximum possible answer?
universalCases.commonFailure: Integer overflow when answer approaches INT_MAX.

arrayCases.checkQuestion: Does solution return correct answer for [x]?
arrayCases.commonFailure: Loop runs 0 times and returns uninitialized value

arrayCases.checkQuestion: Does solution correctly compare/process exactly two elements?
arrayCases.commonFailure: Middle index calculation wrong for even-length arrays

arrayCases.checkQuestion: Does solution work when every element is negative?
arrayCases.commonFailure: Initializing max with 0 instead of INT_MIN. Missing negative-only subarray as valid answer.

arrayCases.case: Mix of positive, negative, and zero
arrayCases.checkQuestion: Does solution handle sign changes correctly?
arrayCases.commonFailure: Absolute value taken incorrectly. Comparison with 0 used as boundary incorrectly.
Division by zero. Log of zero. Modulo by zero.

arrayCases.case: Array contains zeros among other values
arrayCases.checkQuestion: Does solution handle zeros in mixed input without special-casing incorrectly?
arrayCases.commonFailure: Skipping zeros when they should be included. Product becomes zero unexpectedly.

arrayCases.case: Values large enough to cause overflow when multiplied or summed
arrayCases.checkQuestion: Can any two elements multiplied together exceed INT_MAX (2.1 * 10^9)?
arrayCases.commonFailure: int overflow when accumulating sum or product. Use long long.

arrayCases.checkQuestion: Does adding 1 to INT_MAX or subtracting 1 from INT_MIN overflow?
arrayCases.commonFailure: Incrementing INT_MAX wraps to INT_MIN silently in C++.

arrayCases.case: Many duplicates — most elements are the same value
arrayCases.checkQuestion: Does solution perform correctly when majority of elements are identical?
arrayCases.commonFailure: Two pointer gets stuck. Binary search infinite loop on duplicate boundary.

arrayCases.case: Alternating pattern — high low high low
arrayCases.checkQuestion: Does solution correctly process alternating value patterns?
arrayCases.commonFailure: Greedy that assumes monotonicity fails on alternating input.

stringCases.checkQuestion: Does solution handle empty string input without crashing?
stringCases.commonFailure: Accessing s[0] on empty string. Length-1 underflow for unsigned types.

stringCases.checkQuestion: Does solution return correct answer for one-character string?
stringCases.commonFailure: Substring of length 0 returned. Palindrome check incorrect for length 1.

stringCases.checkQuestion: Does solution handle all-same character input?
stringCases.commonFailure: Sliding window fails to slide. KMP failure function loops.

stringCases.case: Only two distinct characters — ababab or aabbaabb
stringCases.checkQuestion: Does solution work with minimal alphabet?
stringCases.commonFailure: Solutions hardcoded for 26-character alphabet behave incorrectly.

stringCases.checkQuestion: Does solution correctly identify or process palindromes?
stringCases.commonFailure: Off-by-one in even vs odd length palindrome expansion.

stringCases.case: String is one character away from palindrome
stringCases.commonFailure: Palindrome check exits early without checking full string.

stringCases.case: String containing only spaces or special characters
stringCases.commonFailure: Character classification assumes only a-z. Space causes word count errors.

stringCases.checkQuestion: Does O(n^2) string algorithm TLE at max length?
stringCases.commonFailure: Naive substring search O(n*m) TLEs at n=10^5.

stringCases.case: String is a repeated pattern — abcabcabc
stringCases.checkQuestion: Does solution correctly identify period or handle repetition?
stringCases.commonFailure: KMP period calculation incorrect for clean repeating strings.

stringCases.checkQuestion: Does solution treat uppercase and lowercase correctly per problem statement?
stringCases.commonFailure: Forgot to convert to lowercase when case-insensitive comparison needed.

treeCases.checkQuestion: Does solution return correct answer for tree with no children?
treeCases.commonFailure: Accessing left or right child without null check. Returning wrong base case.

treeCases.case: Root with one child (left or right only)
treeCases.checkQuestion: Does solution handle tree where one child is null?
treeCases.commonFailure: Assuming both children always exist. Null pointer dereference.

treeCases.checkQuestion: Does recursive solution stack overflow on n=10^5 left-skewed tree?
treeCases.commonFailure: Recursive DFS with depth n causes stack overflow. Use iterative DFS.

treeCases.checkQuestion: Same as left-skewed — does recursion depth exceed stack limit?
treeCases.commonFailure: Same stack overflow risk as left-skewed.

treeCases.checkQuestion: Does solution correctly handle perfectly balanced tree?
treeCases.commonFailure: Usually the easy case — but verify base cases still work.

treeCases.checkQuestion: Does solution distinguish nodes by position not value?
treeCases.commonFailure: BST solutions break when all values equal — duplicate handling.

treeCases.checkQuestion: Does solution initialize path sum with 0 when it should use INT_MIN?
treeCases.commonFailure: Max path sum initialized to 0 — misses all-negative tree.

treeCases.checkQuestion: Does solution consider paths that go through root specifically?
treeCases.commonFailure: Only checking subtree paths, missing root-crossing path.

treeCases.case: Optimal path is entirely within one subtree
treeCases.checkQuestion: Does solution check subtree answers independently of root?
treeCases.commonFailure: Forcing all paths through root — misses subtree-only optimal path.

treeCases.case: Tree with depth up to n (degenerate)
treeCases.checkQuestion: For n=10^5, does recursive solution stay within stack limits?
treeCases.commonFailure: Default stack allows ~10^4 to 10^5 recursive calls. Skewed tree hits this.

graphCases.case: Graph with single node and no edges
graphCases.checkQuestion: Does solution handle isolated single node?
graphCases.commonFailure: Returning 0 components instead of 1. Shortest path to self = 0 not handled.

graphCases.case: Two nodes with no edge between them
graphCases.checkQuestion: Does solution return correct answer when nodes are disconnected?
graphCases.commonFailure: Returning -1 vs infinity vs 0 for unreachable node — inconsistent.

graphCases.case: Two nodes connected by single edge
graphCases.checkQuestion: Does solution handle minimal connected graph?
graphCases.commonFailure: Bidirectional edge added only once in undirected graph.

graphCases.checkQuestion: Does solution handle E = O(V^2) without MLE or TLE?
graphCases.commonFailure: Adjacency matrix for V=10^5 complete graph — 40GB MLE.

graphCases.checkQuestion: Does solution handle nodes unreachable from source?
graphCases.commonFailure: BFS from single source marks some nodes unvisited — answer only covers one component.

graphCases.checkQuestion: Does solution handle self-loops without infinite loop?
graphCases.commonFailure: DFS revisits node via self-loop. Cycle detection false positive.

graphCases.case: Multiple edges between same pair of nodes
graphCases.checkQuestion: Does solution use minimum weight edge between parallel edges?
graphCases.commonFailure: Counting edges when should count pairs. MST uses both parallel edges.

graphCases.checkQuestion: Does solution detect and handle negative cycle correctly?
graphCases.commonFailure: Bellman-Ford runs V-1 rounds but does not check for further relaxation in round V.

graphCases.checkQuestion: Does Dijkstra degenerate to BFS behavior correctly?
graphCases.commonFailure: Usually fine — but verify MST gives correct answer when all weights equal.

graphCases.case: Star graph — one center node connected to all others
graphCases.checkQuestion: Does solution handle hub-spoke topology correctly?
graphCases.commonFailure: Diameter calculation off — all paths go through center.

intervalCases.checkQuestion: Does solution return correct answer (0 or empty) for no intervals?
intervalCases.commonFailure: Accessing index 0 of empty array. Returning uninitialized value.

intervalCases.checkQuestion: Does solution handle one interval correctly?
intervalCases.commonFailure: Merge logic tries to compare with previous — no previous exists.

intervalCases.checkQuestion: Does solution correctly merge or count overlapping pair?
intervalCases.commonFailure: Overlap condition uses > instead of >= for touching intervals.

intervalCases.case: Two intervals that do not overlap
intervalCases.checkQuestion: Does solution correctly keep separate non-overlapping intervals?
Does solution handle fully nested intervals?
intervalCases.commonFailure: Nested interval end time not updated correctly during merge.

intervalCases.case: Two intervals that share exactly one endpoint — [1,3] and [3,5]
intervalCases.checkQuestion: Does your definition of overlap include touching endpoints?
intervalCases.commonFailure: Problem may define [1,3] and [3,5] as overlapping or not — check problem statement.

intervalCases.case: All intervals overlap with each other
intervalCases.checkQuestion: Does solution correctly merge all into single interval?
intervalCases.commonFailure: Loop exits early — merged interval not updated with maximum end time.

intervalCases.checkQuestion: Does solution correctly reduce n identical intervals to one?
intervalCases.commonFailure: Deduplication not handled — returns n intervals instead of 1.

intervalCases.case: Interval where start equals end — [x, x]
intervalCases.checkQuestion: Does solution handle zero-length point intervals?
intervalCases.commonFailure: Interval length = 0 causes division by zero or empty range handling.

intervalCases.checkQuestion: Does solution work when interval values are negative?
intervalCases.commonFailure: Sorting by absolute value instead of actual value.

numericCases.checkQuestion: Does solution handle n=0 or value=0 without division by zero or log(0)?
numericCases.commonFailure: Division by n when n=0. log(0) is undefined.

numericCases.checkQuestion: Does solution handle n=1 correctly (especially for prime checks, factorials)?
numericCases.commonFailure: 1 is not prime but many prime checks return true for 1.

numericCases.checkQuestion: Does solution handle negative input values?
numericCases.commonFailure: Modulo of negative number. Square root of negative. Unsigned type wraps.

numericCases.checkQuestion: Can any multiplication or addition overflow 32-bit integer?
numericCases.commonFailure: a * b where a and b are both up to 10^5 — product is 10^10 which overflows int.

numericCases.checkQuestion: Can any computation overflow even 64-bit integer?
numericCases.commonFailure: Factorial of large n. Power computation without modular arithmetic.

numericCases.checkQuestion: Are all additions, multiplications, and subtractions taken mod 10^9+7?
numericCases.commonFailure: Adding two numbers each just below 10^9+7 — sum overflows before mod applied.

numericCases.checkQuestion: Does solution handle powers of 2 correctly in bit manipulation?
numericCases.commonFailure: n & (n-1) == 0 check for power of 2 fails for n=0.

numericCases.checkQuestion: Does solution correctly identify prime inputs?
numericCases.commonFailure: Sieve off-by-one — forgetting to mark 0 and 1 as not prime.

numericCases.checkQuestion: Does solution handle the case where input equals the modulus?
numericCases.commonFailure: a % MOD when a == MOD gives 0 — may be intended or bug depending on context.

numericCases.checkQuestion: Are floating point comparisons using epsilon tolerance instead of direct equality?
numericCases.commonFailure: Comparing doubles with == — fails due to floating point imprecision.

dpSpecificCases.case: DP on empty array or string
dpSpecificCases.checkQuestion: Does dp[0] or dp[0][0] base case handle empty input?
dpSpecificCases.commonFailure: Accessing dp[-1] when i=0 without boundary check.

dpSpecificCases.checkQuestion: Does transition from dp[1] to dp[0] work correctly?
dpSpecificCases.commonFailure: Loop starts at i=1 but transition tries to access dp[i-2] when i=1.

dpSpecificCases.checkQuestion: Does DP return -1, INT_MAX, or 0 correctly when no solution exists?
dpSpecificCases.commonFailure: Returning INT_MAX/2 instead of -1. Overflow when adding to INT_MAX.

dpSpecificCases.checkQuestion: Does DP correctly return any valid optimal solution?
dpSpecificCases.commonFailure: Non-determinism in tie-breaking causes inconsistent results.

dpSpecificCases.case: Need to reconstruct path not just value
dpSpecificCases.checkQuestion: Is parent/choice array maintained alongside dp array?
dpSpecificCases.commonFailure: Computed optimal value but cannot trace back the actual solution.

binarySearchCases.case: All values in search space are feasible
binarySearchCases.checkQuestion: Does binary search correctly return minimum feasible value?
binarySearchCases.commonFailure: Returns mid-range value instead of lo when all are feasible.

binarySearchCases.case: No value in search space is feasible
binarySearchCases.checkQuestion: Does binary search correctly indicate no solution?
binarySearchCases.commonFailure: Returns lo which is invalid — needs post-loop feasibility check.

binarySearchCases.case: Exactly one value in search space is feasible
binarySearchCases.checkQuestion: Does binary search find the single valid value?
binarySearchCases.commonFailure: Off-by-one in lo/hi update causes skipping the only valid value.

binarySearchCases.case: Answer is at lo or hi boundary
binarySearchCases.checkQuestion: Does binary search correctly return lo or hi when answer is at extreme?
binarySearchCases.commonFailure: Loop terminates before checking boundary value.

binarySearchCases.case: lo + hi overflows int when computing mid
binarySearchCases.checkQuestion: Is mid computed as lo + (hi - lo) / 2 instead of (lo + hi) / 2?
binarySearchCases.commonFailure: (lo + hi) overflows when both are near INT_MAX.
